channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: US Court Directs Revival of Voice of America and Reinstatement of Journalists
item.description: A federal judge in the United States has ordered the US Agency for Global Media (USAGM) to reinstate more than 1,000 Voice of America (VOA) journalists and staff members by 23 March, reversing a major...

item.title: Mismanagement and miscommunication contributed to the collapse of Iran nuclear talks
item.description: The collapse of the Iran nuclear negotiations in early 2026 has been attributed to a combination of mismanagement, lack of expertise, and misunderstandings between the US and Iran. Negotiations had re...

item.title: The Role of AI-Generated Misinformation in Middle Eastern Conflicts
item.description: Since the outbreak of conflict in Iran and the broader Middle East, social media has become a battleground for misinformation and disinformation, often powered by AI. Videos of supposed events, such a...

item.title: Alibaba focuses on AI agents to integrate its business ecosystem and boost revenue
item.description: Alibaba is undergoing a strategic shift in its artificial intelligence approach by prioritising AI agents that can integrate its vast ecosystem of businesses. The company recently launched multiple AI...

item.title: High Repeat Withdrawals from Two-Pot Retirement Savings Signal Financial Pressure
item.description: Since the opening of the third withdrawal window on 1 March 2026, Momentum Corporate reports that over 60% of members accessing their two-pot retirement savings are doing so for the third time. Data f...

item.title: NBA raises concern over judge ordering lawyer to kneel in court
item.description: The Nigerian Bar Association (NBA) don raise wahala over how Justice Mohammed Umar for Federal High Court, Abuja, take handle lawyer Marshall Abubakar. This matter happen on March 16, 2026, when Abuba...

item.title: EACC Orders Former Homa Bay Official to Refund Sh10 Million in Illegally Earned Salaries
item.description: The Ethics and Anti-Corruption Commission (EACC) has successfully recovered Sh10 million from Walter Odhiambo, a former Deputy Director of Health - Preventive and Promotive at Homa Bay County Governme...

item.title: Whitechapel: A Foreign Correspondent Explores Integration Challenges in London's Bangladeshi Enclave
item.description: David Patrikarakos visits Whitechapel in London's Tower Hamlets, an area with a large Bangladeshi Muslim population where 40 per cent of residents are Muslim and 56 per cent Asian per the 2021 Census....

item.title: Rapper Mystikal Pleads Guilty to Third-Degree Rape in Louisiana
item.description: On March 18, 2026, US rapper Mystikal, born Michael Tyler, entered a guilty plea for third-degree rape in a Louisiana state court, concluding a major development in a case that began with his 2022 arr...

item.title: DBM Allocates P8.08 Billion for Development Projects in 800 Barangays Cleared of Insurgency
item.description: The Department of Budget and Management (DBM) has released P8.08 billion to fund priority development projects in more than 800 barangays that have been cleared of insurgency. This funding falls under...

item.title: Zamfara Government Reviews Security Plans Ahead of Sallah Festivities
item.description: The Zamfara State Government don gather high-level meeting with top security agencies dem to review the security situation ahead of the Eid-el Fitr (Sallah) celebrations. The Secretary to the State Go...

item.title: Scottish Parliament votes against assisted dying legislation for terminally ill adults
item.description: The Scottish Parliament has voted against the Assisted Dying for Terminally Ill Adults (Scotland) Bill, which would have allowed terminally-ill, mentally competent adults to seek medical assistance to...

item.title: Supreme Court Rules Lawyers Can Be Disbarred for Misogynistic or Sexist Language
item.description: In the Philippines, lawyers can face disbarment for making misogynistic or sexist remarks, according to past rulings by the Supreme Court. This serves as a reminder that legal practitioners are held t...

item.title: March 2026 Stock Market Holiday Schedule Including Gudi Padwa, Eid, and Navratri
item.description: The stock markets in India have a set of holidays in March 2026, which include major festivals like Gudi Padwa, Eid, and Navratri. Traders, investors, and market enthusiasts should take note that the ...

item.title: Government plans to develop Coast Region into key fruits and vegetables production hub

item.title: Airlines monitor jet fuel supply as Middle East tensions impact global operations
item.description: Australian airlines are keeping close communication with the Transport Minister amid concerns over aviation fuel supplies, as ongoing conflicts in the Middle East disrupt global supply chains. Qantas,...

item.title: House Justice Committee Finds Sufficient Grounds for Sara Duterte Impeachment, Public Hearing Scheduled
item.description: MANILA, Philippines – The House Justice Committee on March 18, 2026, determined that the impeachment complaints filed against Vice President Sara Duterte contain sufficient grounds to proceed, leading...

item.title: Strengthening Democracy and Digital Information Integrity Through Media Literacy in Africa
item.description: Dis na article wey dey yarn how media and information literacy (MIL) fit help promote democracy and maintain information integrity for Africa, especially as elections dey near for Nigeria and Kenya fo...

item.title: Stellenbosch and Golden Arrows clash to secure top-eight league position
item.description: Stellenbosch and Golden Arrows are set for a pivotal PSL clash at King Zwelithini Stadium in Umlazi, Durban, tonight at 7.30pm. Both sides sit level on 24 points, with Arrows just ahead on goal differ...

item.title: Tanzania MPs Call for Protection of Water Projects and Sources
item.description: In Manyara Region, Tanzania, the Parliamentary Standing Committee on Water and Environment is urging citizens to protect water sources and infrastructure to avoid water shortages and other climate cha...
